engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Galant across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary DOHC inline-four powering GLS and VR-4 models; interference design demands timely belt changes.
compatibleVariants: GLS, GLXi, Classic MPI, VR-4 (turbo), Equippe
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing bent valves (interference engine)
Crankwalk on 7-bolt engines (VR-4 models, 60k-100k miles)
Hydraulic lash adjuster noise at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G63 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged evolution of 4G63 for VR-4; sought-after by performance enthusiasts for rally heritage.
commonFailures: Crankwalk thrust bearing failure (7-bolt block, 60k-90k miles)
Turbocharger oil seal leaks and boost creep
Timing belt tensioner wear leading to catastrophic failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G63T Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Stroker version of 4G63 offering more low-end torque for GLXi and estate variants.
commonFailures: Hydraulic lash adjuster tick at idle (common, not critical)
Oil sludge buildup with extended service intervals
Water pump failure causing overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L GDI Petrol
entries.history: Direct injection innovation for fuel economy; notorious for carbon deposits in intake manifold.
commonFailures: Carbon build-up on intake valves causing misfire (30k-60k miles)
High-pressure fuel pump failure
Injector clogging requiring professional cleaning
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G93 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Smooth DOHC V6 for Mk VII Galant; precursor to larger 6A13 in later generations.
commonFailures: Balance shaft belt failure causing timing belt damage
Coil pack failure leading to misfire
Oil leaks from rear main seal
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6A12 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Most common Galant engine in UK; reliable V6 powering Elegance and Sport trims across Mk VIII.
compatibleVariants: V6 Elegance, V6 Sport, Estate V6, Classic V6
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear (interference engine, replace at 60k miles)
Valve cover gasket oil leaks
EGR valve clogging causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6A13 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Twin-Turbo V6 Petrol
entries.history: Flagship twin-turbo V6 for VR-4 Type S; JDM import favourite with 280hp output.
compatibleVariants: VR-4 Type S, VR-4 Type V, Legnum VR-4 (import)
commonFailures: Turbocharger seal failure causing oil consumption
Active Yaw Control differential wear (Type S models)
Intercooler piping cracks under boost pressure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6A13TT Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Earlier V6 option for Mk VII GTi; shared architecture with 6A13 but different head design.
compatibleVariants: GTi, V6 Sport (early), European export models
commonFailures: Cylinder head gasket failure under heat stress
Timing belt wear requiring preventative replacement
Oil pump drive belt failure affecting lubrication
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G73 Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Galant's petrol engines-particularly the 4G63 and 6A13 families-dominate UK replacement demand due to interference design and high-mileage use. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mitsubishi Galant Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Galant GLS, GLXi, V6 Sport, VR-4 (1983-2004, 4G63, 6A12, 6A13, 6G73)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles if not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt deteriorates with age and heat; tensioner failure causes belt jump, bending valves on interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended at 60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reface seats, new gaskets
Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: On a Galant worth £1,500-£3,000, preventative belt replacement is the smart investment. If valves are already bent, a rebuilt engine from our network typically costs less than a dealer head repair and comes with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63 and 6A13 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Galant

problems.rootCause: Insufficient thrust bearing clearance on 7-bolt blocks allows crankshaft end-play, causing bearing wear and eventual seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crankshaft, bearings, seals; machine block if scored
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit forged short block, upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for performance builds; eliminates crankwalk risk permanently

problems.recommendation: Crankwalk is catastrophic-once clutch pedal sticks or knock appears, the engine is finished. For VR-4 owners, a performance-rebuilt 4G63T from a specialist offers the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4G63T VR-4 engines from UK specialists

problems.h4: Balance Shaft Belt Failure - Hidden Risk on 4G63 and 6A12
problems.rootCause: Balance shaft drive belt snaps without warning, jamming timing belt and causing interference engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace balance shaft belt and tensioner during service
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; must be done before failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with balance shaft delete option available
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective after failure; eliminates belt risk

problems.recommendation: Balance shaft failure is silent until it destroys your engine. If buying a used Galant, budget for immediate belt replacement. Post-failure, a rebuilt engine is cheaper than repairing bent valves.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63 engine prices with balance shaft delete option

problems.h4: 4G93 GDI Carbon Build-Up - Direct Injection Drawback
problems.rootCause: Direct injection sprays fuel into cylinder only; intake valves never get fuel wash, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast carbon from valves with walnut media
Replace clogged high-pressure injectors, recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses misfire; injectors wear with carbon exposure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated intake design
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage GDI engines; eliminates carbon cycle

problems.recommendation: GDI engines need proactive maintenance. If misfire persists after cleaning, injector replacement is worthwhile. For cars over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine often offers better value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 4G93 GDI engines with updated intake components

problems.h4: Hydraulic Lash Adjuster Noise - Common but Manageable
problems.rootCause: Oil sludge or wear prevents hydraulic lifters from self-adjusting, causing persistent valve train tick.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, replace faulty hydraulic lifters, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Quietens engine; use quality oil to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded valvetrain components
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates noise permanently; best for high-mileage cars

problems.recommendation: Lifter tick is annoying but rarely catastrophic. Try an engine flush and quality synthetic oil first. If noise persists or mileage is high, a rebuilt engine resolves the issue and refreshes the entire powertrain.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G64 and 6A13 engine prices for your Galant

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Galant
finalCta.paragraph: From timing belt catastrophes to VR-4 crankwalk, Galant engine failures are well-documented-but replacement doesn't have to break the bank. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address the original failure point. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get your Galant back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Galant Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Galant and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Galant is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L four-cylinder powered entry-level Galant variants in the UK, notably the GDI-equipped models from 1996 onward. Its direct injection technology improved fuel economy but introduced carbon build-up challenges requiring proactive maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Carbon deposits on intake valves causing misfire; high-pressure fuel pump wear
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The versatile 2.0L inline-four was the Galant's workhorse across multiple generations, from the Mk V GLS to the VR-4 performance flagship. Its interference design demands strict timing belt discipline to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure (interference); crankwalk on 7-bolt VR-4 engines
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L stroker variant of the 4G63 offered enhanced low-end torque for GLXi and estate models. While robust, it shares the hydraulic lash adjuster sensitivity of its smaller sibling.
commonFailurePoints: Hydraulic lash adjuster tick; oil sludge with extended service intervals
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L V6 family (6A12/6A13/6G73) dominated UK Galant sales from 1992-2004, powering Sport, Elegance and VR-4 variants. Smooth and refined, but interference design requires timely belt replacement to prevent expensive damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt tensioner wear; balance shaft belt failure (6A12); turbo seal leaks (6A13TT)
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant 2.5L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Mitsubishi Galant needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mitsubishi Galant specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mitsubishi Galant engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 4G63 GLS to the latest 6A13TT VR-4.

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant Petrol Engines
items.description: All UK-market Galants were petrol-powered, spanning economical four-cylinders to high-performance twin-turbo V6 units across five generations.
items.descriptor: All UK-market Galants were petrol-powered, spanning economical four-cylinders to high-performance twin-turbo V6 units across five generations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Galant Petrol Engine Families
families: 4G63 - most common replacement engine (GLS/VR-4), timing belt & crankwalk risks
6A13 - dominant V6 for Elegance/Sport variants, reliable with preventative maintenance
4G93 GDI - direct injection efficiency, carbon build-up requires regular cleaning
6A13TT - twin-turbo VR-4 flagship, specialist rebuilds for performance applications
knownFor: Interference engine design demands strict timing belt replacement intervals
VR-4 crankwalk (7-bolt 4G63T) is catastrophic but preventable with upgraded parts
6A13 V6 offers strong reliability when maintained; most common UK replacement
GDI variants need intake cleaning to avoid misfire and power loss
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant Petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Galant Diesel Engines
items.description: No diesel engines were officially offered on the Mitsubishi Galant for the UK market.
items.descriptor: No diesel engines were officially offered on the Mitsubishi Galant for the UK market.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Galant Diesel Engine Families
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Galant Petrol replacement engines

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Galant's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The fifth-generation Galant introduced the legendary 4G63 inline-four to UK buyers, establishing the foundation for future performance variants.
keyChanges: First UK-market Galant with 4G63 2.0L petrol engine
GL and GLX trim levels with engine-specific badging
knownFor: Robust 4G63 block forms basis for VR-4 and Evo engines
Interference design requires timely timing belt changes
Simple SOHC layout aids DIY maintenance and repair
years.cta: Get quotes for 1983-1987 Mitsubishi Galant replacement engines

years.description: The sixth generation launched the iconic VR-4 with turbocharged 4G63T, creating a performance legacy that still drives replacement demand today.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4G63T turbocharged engine for VR-4 variant
Four-wheel drive and four-wheel steering technology debut
DOHC cylinder head upgrade across petrol range
knownFor: 4G63T crankwalk (7-bolt block) is signature failure at 60k-90k miles
VR-4 AYC differential requires specialist knowledge for repairs
High collector value drives premium rebuild demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1992 Mitsubishi Galant VR-4 engines
years.ticker: Galant VR-4 engine replacement • 4G63T crankwalk repair • turbo rebuild cost VR-4

years.description: The seventh generation expanded the Galant range with smooth V6 options, while retaining the 4G63 for mainstream variants.
keyChanges: Introduction of 6A12 2.0L V6 and 6G73 2.5L V6 engines
GTi model with 170hp 6G73 and four-wheel drive (European market)
Facelift in 1994 with revised front end and interior updates
knownFor: Balance shaft belt failure on 6A12 can destroy timing belt
6G73 cylinder head gasket vulnerability under heat stress
4G63 remains reliable workhorse with proper maintenance
years.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Galant 1992-1996 V6 engine prices

years.description: The eighth generation represented the Galant's UK peak, with the 6A13 V6 becoming the most common replacement engine across Elegance and Sport trims.
keyChanges: Introduction of 6A13 2.5L V6 as mainstream engine
Legnum wagon body style added alongside sedan
4G93 GDI direct injection technology for fuel economy focus
knownFor: 6A13 timing belt tensioner wear is primary failure point
4G93 GDI carbon build-up requires walnut blasting maintenance
VR-4 Type S AYC system adds complexity to drivetrain repairs
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2004 Mitsubishi Galant replacement engines
years.ticker: 6A13 engine replacement UK • Galant GDI carbon cleaning • VR-4 Type S engine rebuild

years.description: The final VR-4 production run featured the potent 6A13TT twin-turbo V6, creating enduring demand for specialist performance rebuilds among UK enthusiasts.
keyChanges: 6A13TT twin-turbo V6 rated at 280hp (Japanese voluntary limit)
Active Yaw Control (AYC) and ASC traction systems standard on Type S
Limited UK grey imports via Ralliart type-approval in 2000
mainEngines: 6A13TT - 2.5L twin-turbo V6 petrol (VR-4 Type S/Type V)
knownFor: Turbocharger seal failure causes oil consumption and smoke
AYC differential wear requires specialist calibration
High collector value justifies premium rebuild investment
years.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Galant VR-4 twin-turbo engine availability

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Galant Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Galant in the UK?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi Galant used several petrol engines across its UK production run, primarily the 4G63 inline-four and 6A13 V6 families. Compatibility depends on generation, chassis code and variant.
keyPoints: 4G63 fits GLS, GLXi and VR-4 variants (1983-2004)
6A13 V6 powers Elegance and Sport trims (1996-2004)
Engine mounts and ECU differ between Mk V-VIII generations
Always verify chassis code (E10/E30/E50/EA) before ordering
items.cta: Compare compatible Mitsubishi Galant engine prices by variant

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Galant engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Galant engines typically range from £750 to £3,800 depending on engine code, with 4G63 and 6A13 at the lower end and VR-4 turbo units commanding premium pricing.
keyPoints: 4G63 rebuilt: £750-£1,800 supply only
6A13 V6 rebuilt: £800-£2,400 supply only
6A13TT VR-4 rebuilt: £1,500-£3,800 supply only
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour depending on location
items.cta: Get tailored Mitsubishi Galant engine quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is the 4G63 engine in the Galant VR-4 reliable?
items.answer: The 4G63 is robust when maintained, but 7-bolt VR-4 versions suffer crankwalk failure between 60k-90k miles without upgraded thrust bearings.
keyPoints: Crankwalk causes clutch pedal sticking and eventual seizure
Preventative short block rebuild costs £1,100-£2,100 specialist
Rebuilt 4G63T with forged components eliminates crankwalk risk
Regular oil changes with quality synthetic reduce bearing wear
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63T VR-4 engines with crankwalk prevention

items.question: Why does my Galant 6A13 V6 have a timing belt noise?
items.answer: Timing belt tensioner wear is the most common cause of noise on 6A13 engines; replace belt and tensioner at 60k miles to prevent interference engine damage.
keyPoints: 6A13 is interference design; belt failure bends valves
Tensioner replacement costs £280-£450 specialist labour
Full belt kit with water pump recommended at same interval
Ignoring noise risks £2,000+ cylinder head repair costs
items.cta: Get quotes for 6A13 timing belt replacement and engine rebuild

items.question: Can I replace a Galant engine myself?
items.answer: While mechanically possible, Galant engine replacement requires specialist tools and knowledge of AWD/AYC systems on VR-4 models; professional supply & fit is recommended.
keyPoints: VR-4 AYC differential needs calibration after engine removal
Engine hoist and transmission jack essential for safe removal
ECU coding may be required for GDI and V6 variants
Professional fit includes warranty on labour and components
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Galant engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Do you warranty Mitsubishi Galant replacement engines?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists, with extended options available.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Claim process handled directly with supplying specialist
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Extended 24-month warranties available on remanufactured units
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Mitsubishi Galant rebuilt engines

items.question: What causes 4G93 GDI engines to misfire in the Galant?
items.answer: Carbon build-up on intake valves is the primary cause of misfire on 4G93 GDI engines; walnut blasting cleaning restores performance every 30k miles.
keyPoints: Direct injection sprays fuel into cylinder only, not valves
Carbon deposits restrict airflow causing rough idle and power loss
Walnut blasting costs £180-£320 specialist labour
Preventative fuel system cleaner additives offer limited benefit
items.cta: Find rebuilt 4G93 GDI engines with updated intake components

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Galant engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Galant engines regularly exceed 150,000 miles; timing belt adherence and oil quality are the biggest factors in longevity.
keyPoints: 4G63 and 6A13 can reach 200k miles with preventative care
Timing belt replacement at 60k miles prevents catastrophic failure
Synthetic oil changes every 5k-7k miles reduce wear significantly
Address minor leaks early to avoid secondary damage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engines for high-mileage Mitsubishi Galant variants

Instant quotes compare prices from multiple suppliers

All suppliers rated and reviewed by real Galant owners
items.cta: Get free Mitsubishi Galant engine quotes from trusted UK suppliers

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Galant?
items.answer: For Galants in good body and interior condition, a rebuilt engine often extends usable life cost-effectively versus purchasing a replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 6A13 costs £800-£2,400 vs £3k+ for comparable used car
Warranty protection reduces long-term ownership risk
Supply & fit bundles labour for predictable total cost
Consider sentimental value and known service history
